title: Circular Phase Encoding via Inversion and Radix-12 Projection:  A Deterministic Method for Indexing Cyclic Offset Residues
author: Lesperance, Joel Michael
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Periodic systems generate normalized phase offsets that accumulate under modular closure. This paper introduces a deterministic encoding procedure for cyclic offsets that operates without direct reference to velocity or force quantities. The method consists of: (1) normalization to unit phase, (2) inversion or percent-scaling where appropriate, (3) fixed-resolution integer encoding, and (4) radix-12 projection with optional lattice reduction. The procedure functions as a circular-phase indexing operator suitable for rotational and orbital systems. The method is representation-sensitive and must be applied under declared normalization and precision constraints. When properly constrained, it provides a reproducible signature mapping of cyclic offsets.

When properly constrained, it provides a reproducible signature mapping of cyclic offsets.
